purpose: Adds a guard in hci_cmd_sync_queue to return -ENODEV when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing queuing commands after the device has been unregistered and avoiding a potential use‑after‑free.

The commit removes a stray <asm/termios.h> include from drivers/tty/n_hdlc.c, which is unnecessary because linux/tty.h already pulls in linux/termios.h. This cleanup eliminates an extraneous header dependency.
This commit updates the perf-c2c documentation to include the 'N/A' metric for store operations, adding it to the store reference tables and adjusting the associated descriptions.

This commit fixes several bugs in the hugetlb VMA lock handling logic. It addresses a race in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio, prevents double freeing of the VMA lock when vm_area_dup copies the lock pointer during VMA splitting, ensures a VMA lock is allocated for all VM_MAYSHARE mappings, and updates the unmap and sharing code to correctly lock, unlock, and free the VMA lock. The changes also modify the page_table_shareable check to require the VMA lock and adjust the lock free logic to use the new shareable flag helper. Overall, the patch corrects existing defects and adds safeguards against future bugs.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding for the Realtek rt5682s codec by adding two new supply properties, AVDD‑supply and MICVDD‑supply, documenting their purpose and making them required. It also shows an example of how to reference these supplies in a device tree source.
Adds a missing iounmap() call in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function to free a memory-mapped resource that was not released, thereby fixing a resource leak bug.
The commit removes an unused work_struct field from the mlx5e_async_ctx structure used in the kTLS implementation, simplifying the data structure.

The commit changes the type of the key_idx field in cfg80211_auth_request from an unsigned 8‑bit integer (u8) to a signed 8‑bit integer (s8). This allows the field to hold the value -1, which is used when the key is NULL to indicate that the key index is not applicable. The change is made to avoid static analysis warnings (e.g., from smatch) and to prevent potential misuse of the field as an unsigned value.

The commit removes the commented CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS line from two RISC‑V defconfig files, effectively reordering the configuration entries to match the order used by savedefconfig. It does not alter any functional behavior.

The commit introduces a helper inline function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() that checks whether the MCP251XFD controller is operating in CAN‑FD or listen‑only mode. It replaces several inline bit‑mask checks scattered across the driver with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies the RTC selftest to detect when the RTC device file is missing and skip the tests instead of asserting a failure. It adds checks for self->fd == -1 && errno == ENOENT, calls SKIP, and then asserts the file descriptor is valid.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c, eliminating a dead store that was never read and cleaning up a clang warning.

This commit removes the mandatory dependency of the rcuscale scalability test on RCU Tasks. It edits the Kconfig to stop selecting TASKS_RCU for the RCU_SCALE_TEST option, updates rcuscale.c to include the tasks_ops structure only when CONFIG_TASKS_RCU is enabled, and adjusts the self‑tests configuration to use CONFIG_FORCE_TASKS_RCU instead of CONFIG_TASKS_RCU. The result is that rcuscale can now be built (either built‑in or as a module) without pulling in the RCU Tasks subsystem, reducing kernel size and simplifying tracing.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase "be split" from a comment in arch/x86/kvm/mmu/spte.h, cleaning up the documentation.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the dcn315 clock table using dcfclk values. The original change caused an underflow when dcfclk states were disabled, leading to a failure. By reverting the change, the commit restores the prior behavior and prevents the underflow until a better solution is implemented.
Adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU to the Mali‑Bifrost device‑tree bindings YAML, enabling the kernel to recognize this GPU.

This commit renames the struct type used for PCIe root port objects from `pcie_port` to `dw_pcie_rp` throughout the dwc PCIe driver code. The change updates all references, typedefs, and function signatures to use the new name, improving consistency with the existing `dw_` prefix used for other DW PCIe core structures.

The commit changes the format string in a debug print from "%#06hx" to "%#06x" to match the promoted type of the argument, eliminating a clang -Wformat warning and preventing potential undefined behavior.
The commit corrects the branch range check in MIPS jump_label code by casting the upper bound to long, ensuring a signed comparison and preventing false warnings for negative offsets.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from the SetHwReg8188EU function and the corresponding enum value, replacing calls to that case with direct calls to rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd. This simplifies the HAL interface by eliminating an unused or redundant variable.

The commit changes the visibility of a symbol in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c from a non-static const array to a static const array, thereby fixing sparse warnings about undeclared symbols and improving code quality by limiting the symbol’s scope.

This commit removes incorrect interrupt flag specifications from the MDSS device tree bindings for MSM display controllers, correcting the binding to reflect that the MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell and should not include flags. It updates the YAML documentation for msm8998 and qcm2290 to drop the IRQ_TYPE_* values.

The commit renames and extends the handling of control references to support arrays. It changes the field names from dynamic array specific to generic array, updates allocation logic, and adjusts related functions to use the new array semantics. This prepares the V4L2 control subsystem to allow drivers to change array sizes at runtime.

Adds support for additional Qualcomm SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and PMIC models (PMK8350, PM8350, PM8350C, PM8350B, PMR735A, PMR735B, PM8450, PM8010) to the socinfo driver, expanding the driver’s ability to identify these devices.

The commit changes the rtl8xxxu driver to correctly map all 4‑bit chip cut values (0–15) to the corresponding letters A–P. Previously only values 0–4 were handled and any higher value was reported as "unknown". The patch replaces the switch with a simple calculation and updates the format string accordingly.

The commit removes the redundant .is_enabled field from the PRCMU clock structure and deletes the associated enable/disable/is_enabled functions and references. The core clock framework already tracks enable counts, so this cleanup simplifies the driver.

This commit cleans up the RISC‑V mm init code by removing redundant #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL blocks. It moves the pt_ops macro into a single conditional block and groups other XIP‑specific declarations together, reducing the number of conditional compilation directives.
The commit modifies the device tree for the SM6350 Lenovo Xperia to enable the QUP and GPI DMA hardware blocks by setting their status to "okay", thereby allowing future I2C nodes to be added.

This commit cleans up comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: it replaces a vague comment with a clearer one, corrects a typo in the word "calibration", and moves comment text to a separate line to satisfy coding style guidelines. No functional code changes are made.

The commit replaces a busy-wait loop that could spin forever when the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired with a check that logs an error and returns. This prevents an infinite loop that would occur if the hardware is surprise-removed.

This commit refactors the pcm3168a ASoC codec driver to replace legacy "master_mode" terminology with the newer "provider_mode" terminology, updating related constants, variable names, and error messages accordingly. It does not add new functionality or fix a known bug, but improves code readability and consistency with modern ASoC conventions.
The commit removes a redundant macro definition for IS_SWAPFILE that was never used because the macro is always defined elsewhere. The change deletes five lines of code that provide a no-op fallback.

The commit implements GTP echo request handling by adding support for echo responses in both GTPv0 and GTPv1. It introduces new constants, structures, and a restart counter attribute, and updates the GTP device initialization and netlink interface to expose this counter. The added logic sends an echo response when no userspace daemon is present, ensuring compliance with the GTP specification.

This commit replaces uses of the cpumask_next_* family of functions with the more appropriate cpumask_first_* equivalents where the logic is trivial (e.g., when the start index is -1). It also adds a new helper, cpumask_first_zero(), to provide the missing functionality that was previously accessed via cpumask_next_zero(-1, …). The changes are purely a refactor and performance improvement; no new features are introduced and no existing bugs are directly fixed.

The commit removes the call to the firmware IOVAR "dtim_assoc" in the brcmfmac driver. Previously, after roaming, the driver attempted to query TIM information via this IOVAR, which is no longer supported by the firmware and always failed, producing repeated error messages. By eliminating this call, the driver no longer attempts the unsupported operation, eliminating the error logs.

The commit adds a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the Intel ASoC sound subsystem, enabling support for the alc5682 headset codec on a platform that lacks a speaker amplifier. It updates the board ID table and the ACPI match table to include this new board, effectively adding new hardware support.

Adds a check in io_sendmsg_zc to return -EOPNOTSUPP when the socket does not support zerocopy, fixing a bug where unsupported sockets incorrectly allowed zerocopy sendmsg and preventing future failures.

The commit modifies the Sphinx kernel documentation preamble to guard against loading the xeCJK package when it is not installed. It adds a boolean flag and checks for the existence of xeCJK.sty before invoking \\usepackage{xeCJK}. This prevents a build error on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is missing, and updates a note to reflect the new condition.

The commit modifies the checkpatch.pl script to suppress false‑positive warnings for macros that start with "case". It adds a regex to ignore lines beginning with "case" when checking for complex macro values. This fixes a bug in the linting tool that incorrectly flagged legitimate "case" macros used in kernel drivers.

This commit replaces the legacy simple-KMS helpers in the tiny DRM driver with the newer atomic helpers. It removes empty CRTC helper functions, adds an unconditional call to drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state(), and updates the driver’s plane, CRTC, encoder, and connector initialisation to use the atomic helper infrastructure. The change is purely a refactor – no new functionality is added and the observable behaviour of the driver remains the same.

This commit introduces a new JPEG decode worker interface for the MT8195 platform, enabling three hardware decoders to run in parallel. It adds helper functions for hardware selection, state management, and worker logic, extends the device structure with new fields and synchronization primitives, and registers a new device variant for the decoder. The changes also adjust IRQ handling and timeout logic to support the new worker model.

This commit removes a number of VPRINTK() debug logging calls from the libata subsystem and also removes the helper function ata_scsi_dump_cdb(), which was used to dump SCSI command descriptors. The removed debug prints were considered redundant because the same information is already available via tracepoints or SCSI tracing. The change is purely a cleanup and performance improvement, not adding new functionality or fixing a known bug.

This commit adds support for using AMD GPU zstate (a low-power mode) during the extended vertical blank period that can be gained when the display is in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It introduces a new debug option and capability flag for zstate support, adds a helper function to check if extended blank is supported, and extends the stream update structure with a crtc_timing_adjust field. The commit updates the core DC logic to set this flag, copies the timing adjustment into streams, and modifies bandwidth optimization and DML calculations to program extended blank and compute an optimized minimum destination Y start. It also updates hardware sequence code for DCN20/31 to program the extended blank register and adds the necessary function pointers and struct fields. Overall, it implements a new feature that enables zstate during extended vblank when the conditions are met.

The commit changes the type of the third parameter of the hqd_destroy callback from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type so that the function pointer prototype matches the actual implementations, eliminating a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure.
Corrects the property name in the STM32 pinctrl device tree binding from 'reset' to 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to enforce strict property validation.
Adds a conditional compiler flag to suppress array-bounds warnings in the ath9k driver for GCC 12, preventing noisy warnings without altering functionality.

The commit replaces a call to pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the PRNG driver for Allwinner sun8i-ss, removing an unnecessary pm_runtime_put_noidle on error. This simplifies the runtime power‑management handling for the device.

The commit reorganizes io_uring fixed‑file handling by moving the installation and removal logic into the filetable subsystem, changing helper functions to accept an io_ring_ctx instead of an io_kiocb, and adding a dedicated io_fixed_fd_remove helper. It updates callers in openclose.c and rsrc.c to use the new signatures. No new functionality is introduced, and the changes are purely structural and API‑cleaning.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU to split the previously single G1/G2 node into two independent nodes (g1 and g2). It changes the compatible strings to allow separate identifiers, reduces the reg, interrupts, and clocks arrays to single entries, and updates examples accordingly. This enables independent power‑domain control via the vpu‑blk‑ctrl driver.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro, which was only referenced once inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and inlines its contents directly into IWL_DEVICE_BZ. This simplifies the code by eliminating an unnecessary macro.
